chunk_summary: Guidelines for outpatient follow-up of Moderate Acute Malnutrition (MAM) include a checklist for monitoring anthropometry, feeding, morbidity, and supplement use, with escalation criteria for referral based on clinical deterioration or danger signs.
learning_objectives: Understand the essential components of the MAM follow-up visit checklist.
Identify escalation criteria for referral or admission during MAM follow-up.
Learn how to assess and document anthropometry, feeding, and morbidity in MAM cases.
Recognize the importance of WHO high-risk criteria in guiding follow-up frequency and referral thresholds.
key_facts: Anthropometry includes weight, height/length, oedema check, MUAC, and WHZ/WLZ with repeat if borderline.
Immediate referral is required for nutritional oedema, danger signs, or clinical deterioration.
Weight loss or falling MUAC during follow-up is a red flag requiring reassessment and possible referral.
State protocols in India use complications or weight loss to trigger referral to Nutrition Rehabilitation Centres (NRC).
WHO high-risk criteria suggest more frequent contact and lower thresholds for investigation and referral.
reference_quote: State protocols summarized in Indian compendia explicitly use complications or weight loss to trigger referral to facility-based centres such as Nutrition Rehabilitation Centres (NRC).
document_key_topics: Escalation criteria for referral and admission
Checklist for anthropometry, feeding, and morbidity monitoring
